title: T7: Daily workers' review digest — day-windowed end-of-day rollup over engine/store
prompt: David ends the day asking 'what did the workers do today?' — status.py is a now-snapshot and nothing windows engine/store/ by day. Build engine/workers_digest.py: a stdlib-only, strictly READ-ONLY, day-windowed rollup — settled done/ tickets joined with results/ self-reports and audit.jsonl attribution (worker + session, last audit entry wins, hand-closed tickets rendered gracefully), needs-decision items opened in the window, still-running tickets, and a queue snapshot — local-calendar-day window, terminal/--md/--json renderers, --write persisting engine/digests/<date>.md (deliberately OUTSIDE store/), --store fixture override, exit 0 on an empty day. FIRST run the overlap gate: if a day-windowed factory rollup already exists (in engine/, a status.py date mode, or a project-digest surface), park to needs-decision/ instead of building — complement, don't replace. Reuse status.py's done_outcome by import; fixture-prove windowing/joins/read-only before real-store runs; document in engine/README.md; no cron/scheduling; never edit existing engine modules or touch project-digest. Read and execute the war game at backlog/wargames/T7-05-daily-workers-digest.md. Follow its moves in order; on any failure signal apply the counter-move; on any abort condition stop and write needs-decision/.
verify: engine/workers_digest.py exists and parses; `python3 engine/workers_digest.py --help`, `python3 engine/workers_digest.py` (today — empty day legal, prints 'no worker activity'), `python3 engine/workers_digest.py --date 2026-07-04`, and `python3 engine/workers_digest.py --json | python3 -m json.tool` all exit 0; `python3 engine/workers_digest.py --write` creates engine/digests/<today>.md; `python3 engine/status.py` still exits 0. A fixture run demonstrated: day-window exclusion, last-audit-entry worker attribution, hand-closed/no-dispatch-record rendering, blocked-on-you + still-running + queue sections, orphan-result tolerance, empty-day exit 0, and byte-identical store dirs after runs (read-only). Negative: git shows no existing engine/*.py modified, nothing new written under engine/store/ (worker self-report results JSON excepted), no engine/store/digests/ created, no cron/launchd entry added, project-digest repo untouched. engine/README.md gains a Workers' digest section. Legitimate alternate exit: Abort A1/A3 with needs-decision/wg-t7-05-daily-workers-digest.json naming the overlap or foreign work found and NO code committed.
